mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate above 5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods are positive in >60% of cases or UNRATE's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining housing starts (HOUST) signals supply-side bottlenecks and weak demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in interaction-driven scenarios show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.05) or individual proxies dominate predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals weak consumer demand, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-FEDFUNDS regimes show positive correlation with RSAFS or Rank IC < 0.02 in interaction terms.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving construction activity and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in HOUST-up periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or HOUST's predictive power collapses (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month CPI inflation spike above 2% signals central bank intervention risks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPI>2% periods are positive in >50% of cases or CPI's predictive power drops below Rank IC 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in interaction scenarios show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or individual proxies dominate predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During GDP growth (GDPC1 > 1% QoQ), declining retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weak consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in GDP-growth regimes show positive correlation with RSAFS or interaction terms fail to achieve Rank IC > 0.03.
